summary: Record reusable automation or repo hygiene follow-ups discovered while executing mechanism run run-20260422-auto-improve-decision-record-fallback-retry3-linux-tmp.

observations.surface: manual run_mechanism_experiment invocation on WSL-backed /mnt/c vaults
observations.problem: Comparable-run retries launched with the inherited Windows TEMP/TMP environment created disposable workspaces under `/mnt/c/Users/Administrator/AppData/Local/Temp`, and those retries stalled for minutes in `p9_client_rpc` during run capture. Re-running the same experiment with `TMPDIR=/tmp TEMP=/tmp TMP=/tmp` moved the workspace to a Linux-native temp directory and the run completed normally.
observations.why_it_matters: Without a documented Linux temp override, operators can misread WSL 9p filesystem stalls as mechanism regressions or flaky promotion logic and waste iterations on the wrong layer.
observations.automation_candidate: Add a small WSL-safe helper wrapper or Make target for manual mechanism experiments that exports `TMPDIR=/tmp TEMP=/tmp TMP=/tmp` before invoking the experiment runner.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-10 KST: `make run-mechanism-experiment-linux-tmp MECHANISM_RUN_ARGS='...'` now wraps `ops.scripts.run_mechanism_experiment` with `TMPDIR=/tmp TEMP=/tmp TMP=/tmp`, README documents the WSL-safe manual run path, and `tests/test_makefile_static_gates.py` pins the target contract.
